nodes.context: A criminal gang based in southwest Philadelphia, involved in prostitution, extortion, labor racketeering, and narcotics.
A group of major bootleggers during Prohibition, aligned with the Philadelphia faction.
A New York-based Mafia family involved in racketeering and other criminal activities.

edges.evidence_quote: he was a member of the " Big Seven " aligned with the Philadelphia faction along with Waxey Gordon and Irving Blitz
edges.context: Nig Rosen, as head of the 69th Street Gang, was a member of the Big Seven.

edges.evidence_quote: Rosen became partners with Gaetano "Tommy" Lucchese after buying the Sweet Valley Improvement Company which was used by the Lucchese crime family to ship clothing out of New York's garment district.
edges.context: Nig Rosen, head of the 69th Street Gang, became partners with the Lucchese crime family through a business venture.
